claim_text: We used the RadImageNet model,37 a ResNet50 pre-trained on 1.4 million labeled medical images (including 670 000 MRI images) trained in a supervised manner, to compute a RadImageNet Feature Distance (RINFD).

sub_claims.text: vandersluijs2023 introduces / describes the RadImageNet model: a ResNet50 pre-trained in a supervised manner on 1.4 million labeled medical images (including 670 000 MRI images), and supports its use to compute a RadImageNet Feature Distance (RINFD).

evidence.section: content.txt + sections/*.txt (whole paper, all 10 sections + figures/index.json)
evidence.snippet: Exhaustive case-insensitive ripgrep across content.txt and every sections/*.txt file for the terms 'RadImageNet', 'radimagenet', 'ResNet50', 'ResNet-50', 'resnet50', 'RINFD', 'Feature Distance', '1.4 million', '1,400,000', '1.4M', '670 000', '670,000', '670k', '670K', 'Mei', 'mei2022', 'mei et al', and 'ImageNet' returns ZERO matches. The strings 'RadImageNet', 'ResNet', 'RINFD', '1.4 million', '670 000', and 'Mei' do not appear anywhere in the extracted text of vandersluijs2023.

'In this work, we address these challenges by introducing the first large-scale domain-specific variational autoencoder (VAE) designed for compression of high-resolution medical images.' — vandersluijs2023's contribution is a VAE-based image compressor, NOT a supervised classifier and NOT a feature-distance metric.

'Motivated by prior work on large-scale neural compressors (Rombach et al., 2022), we learn functions g and h using a fully convolutional VAE with a combination of a perceptual loss, a patch-based adversarial objective, and a penalty based on the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence. We train six neural compressors with varying values of f (4, 8, and 16) and C (1, 4, and 16).' — the model architecture is a fully-convolutional VAE, not a ResNet50 classifier.

'The training dataset D consists of X-ray data from two modalities: chest X-rays and full-field digital mammograms (FFDM)... MIMIC-CXR, CANDID-PTX, EMBED, CSAW-CC, RSNA Mammography, VinDr-Mammo, INBreast, and CMMD. The final dataset comprises 1,021,356 images.' — the training corpus is ~1.02M chest X-ray + mammogram images (X-ray modality only). It is NOT 1.4 million images, contains NO MRI, and is unsupervised/self-supervised VAE training (perceptual + adversarial + KL), NOT a supervised labeled-class corpus of 670 000 MRI images.

'we evaluate image fidelity by using standard perceptual quality metrics... We report Fréchet Inception Distance (FID), peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR), and multi-scale structural similarity index measure (MS-SSIM). Since FID is computed using an Inception V3 network (Szegedy et al., 2015) that is not adapted for medical images, we introduce two variants based on CLIP and BiomedCLIP (Radford et al., 2021; Zhang et al., 2023; Chambon et al., 2022).' — the closest 'feature-distance' construct in vandersluijs2023 is FID-CLIP and FID-BiomedCLIP (FID variants computed with CLIP / BiomedCLIP backbones), NOT a RadImageNet ResNet50 feature distance.

figures_checked.question: (not consulted) Per figures/index.json caption, this is Figure 1: 'We train a neural compressor that better preserves diagnostic features in medical images (e.g. fracture shown in yellow).' — a chest X-ray comparison panel for the VAE compressor; not RadImageNet content.
figures_checked.vision_response: Not consulted; caption metadata establishes it is the VAE compressor's reconstructed-vs-source X-ray comparison, with no RadImageNet, ResNet50, or feature-distance content.

figures_checked.question: (not consulted) Per figures/index.json caption, this is Figure 2: 'Results from expert reader study.' — a Likert-scale reader-study result chart, not RadImageNet content.
figures_checked.vision_response: Not consulted; caption metadata establishes it is a reader-study results chart with no RadImageNet, ResNet50, or feature-distance content.

figures_checked.question: (not consulted) Per figures/index.json this image has no caption and no TEI match; the surrounding text on page 1 is exclusively about the VAE compressor.
figures_checked.vision_response: Not consulted; surrounding extracted text is uniformly about the VAE compressor, so this orphan figure cannot plausibly contain RadImageNet content not mentioned anywhere in the prose.

sub_claims.claim_value: RadImageNet = ResNet50, supervised, pre-trained on 1.4M labeled medical images including 670 000 MRI; basis for RINFD
sub_claims.nuance: vandersluijs2023 contains NO RadImageNet content of any kind. It is a Med-VAE / neural-compression paper (the work commonly cited as 'Med-VAE'): chest X-ray + full-field digital mammogram VAE with ~1.02M training images, evaluated with FID/FID-CLIP/FID-BiomedCLIP/PSNR/MS-SSIM and an expert reader study. No ResNet50, no MRI, no 670 000 figure, no 1.4M figure, no supervised labeled-class training, no feature-distance metric named RINFD. The literal cite marker on this manuscript sentence is '37' (mei2022 / RadImageNet); ref #50 (vandersluijs2023) appears to have been over-tagged onto this sentence by paragraph-level multi-cite extraction and does not actually support any aspect of this RadImageNet sentence.

phrasings_tried: ResNet50 / ResNet-50 / resnet50 (architecture, all common variants)
medical images (broad probe — returns only VAE-context hits)
Mei / mei2022 / Mei et al (indirect-attribution probe to the actual RadImageNet primary source)
natural image (probe — returns only SD-VAE-baseline hits, not RadImageNet)
attestation.closest_adjacent: vandersluijs2023 is about a domain-specific Variational Autoencoder (VAE) for medical-image *compression* (commonly known as 'Med-VAE'). Its training corpus is 1,021,356 *X-ray* images: chest X-rays from MIMIC-CXR and CANDID-PTX, plus full-field digital mammograms from EMBED, CSAW-CC, RSNA Mammography, VinDr-Mammo, INBreast, and CMMD (content.txt L12 [p2]). The architecture is a fully convolutional VAE trained with a perceptual loss, a patch-based adversarial loss, and a KL-divergence penalty (content.txt L11 [p2]) — i.e. self-supervised reconstruction, NOT supervised classification on labeled classes. Evaluation uses 5 fine-grained classification tasks (probing the VAE's latents with HRNet linear-probe), an expert reader study on 50 reconstructed chest X-rays, and perceptual quality metrics: FID (Inception V3), FID-CLIP, FID-BiomedCLIP, PSNR, and MS-SSIM (content.txt L13–L16, L23). The closest 'feature distance' construct in this paper is FID-CLIP / FID-BiomedCLIP — *a non-RadImageNet variant of FID* — not RINFD. There is no MRI in the corpus, no 670 000 / 1.4M figure, no ResNet, and no RadImageNet.
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: vandersluijs2023 does NOT cite RadImageNet, Mei et al., or any 'RadImageNet' / 'ResNet50' / 'ImageNet' work. Its visible references (extracted in-text) are: Huda & Abrahams 2015; Freire et al. 2022; Tan & Le 2019; Rombach et al. 2022 (Stable Diffusion / SD VAE); Dubois et al. 2021; Dupont et al. 2022; Tellez et al. 2019, 2020; Sushmit et al. 2019; European Society of Radiology 2011; Shannon 1948; Johnson et al. 2019 (MIMIC-CXR); Feng et al. 2021 (CANDID-PTX); Jeong et al. 2022 (EMBED); Sorkhei et al. 2021 (CSAW-CC); RSNA 2023; Nguyen et al. 2022 (VinDr-Mammo); Moreira et al. 2012 (INBreast); Cai et al. 2023 (CMMD); Halabi et al. 2019; Nagy et al. 2022; Wang et al. 2020 (HRNet); Zhang et al. 2016 (linear-probe); Szegedy et al. 2015 (Inception V3 / FID); Radford et al. 2021 (CLIP); Zhang et al. 2023 (BiomedCLIP); Chambon et al. 2022. None of these is RadImageNet (Mei et al. 2022, Radiology AI 4(5):e210315). There is no indirect chain by which the cited RadImageNet/RINFD specification could be reached from vandersluijs2023.
attestation.out_of_context_check: STRONG out-of-context / over-tagged-citation signal. The literal in-text citation marker on the manuscript sentence is '37', which corresponds to mei2022 (the RadImageNet paper). The bib entry vandersluijs2023 is ref #50 in the manuscript and was attached to this sentence by claim-extractor over-tagging at the paragraph level (paragraph 48 contains multiple sentences and ref #50 is cited elsewhere in the paragraph for the Med-VAE / FID-Med usage). vandersluijs2023 itself is a completely different paper: a Med-VAE neural-compression manuscript (chest X-ray + mammogram, ~1.02M images, fully-convolutional VAE, FID/FID-CLIP/FID-BiomedCLIP/PSNR/MS-SSIM evaluation). It contains zero RadImageNet content, zero ResNet50, zero MRI, zero RINFD, and never mentions Mei et al. 2022. Recommended adjudicator framing: this is a CITED_OUT_OF_CONTEXT / over-tagged-citation case (the claim-extractor's paragraph-level multi-cite assignment is the bug) rather than a substantive misattribution by the authors of the manuscript, since the literal numbered cite marker '37' on this sentence does point at the correct source (mei2022).
